assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can install local tooling.
No Slack user account, development workspace, Slack CLI login, or app exist yet.
Workspace governance will allow the developer to create and install apps without an unresolved admin approval queue.

developer_goal: Create Slack access for development, run the official Bolt getting-started app via the Slack CLI, and confirm it responds to hello and a button click.

first_success.outcome: The starter Bolt app runs locally, is installed into a development workspace, and responds to a hello message and button click in Slack.
first_success.observable_signal: After slack run, sending hello and clicking Click Me produces the documented starter-app replies.

route_selection.surface: Slack developer docs quickstart with Slack CLI and Bolt
route_selection.rule: Prefer the vendor-recommended CLI and Bolt route; language tabs remain peer implementations of the same terminal.
route_selection.selected: Slack CLI with Bolt for Python quickstart

candidates.name: Slack CLI with Bolt for Python
candidates.reason: The quickstart recommends the CLI and Bolt for simplicity; Python is the first language tab and declares the same hello / Click Me success.

candidates.name: Slack CLI with Bolt for JavaScript
candidates.reason: Equivalent language tab with the same CLI flow and terminal; Python is listed first under the recommended route.

candidates.name: Browser app-settings / manifest route
candidates.reason: The quickstart presents browser app settings as an alternative and recommends the CLI for the smoothest experience.

required_path.action: Create a Slack user account and a development workspace (or sandbox) usable for app creation.

required_fields.label: Slack account and workspace creation fields
required_fields.notes: The quickstart assumes a workspace exists and recommends developer sandboxes; public get-started HTML did not expose the current signup field set.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can authenticate into a workspace that permits app development.

required_path.action: Install the Slack CLI and confirm it with slack version.
required_path.observable_result: The Slack CLI reports a version.

required_path.action: Run slack login and authenticate the CLI to the development workspace.
required_path.observable_result: The CLI is authenticated for subsequent create and run commands.

required_path.action: Create the Bolt for Python starter project from the documented template and enter its directory.
required_path.observable_result: A local project with requirements.txt and Slack CLI config exists.

required_path.action: Create and activate a Python virtual environment, then install requirements.
required_path.observable_result: Project dependencies are installed in the active virtual environment.

required_path.action: Run slack run and choose Create a new app for the intended workspace.
required_path.observable_result: The Bolt app reports that it is running.

required_path.action: Open a DM with the app or invite it to a public channel, send hello, then click Click Me.
required_path.observable_result: The app replies to hello and to the button click as documented.

prerequisites.description: Python 3.7 or later and Git are documented for the selected Python CLI route.
A Slack workspace where development can happen; Slack recommends a developer sandbox.

external_gates.description: Slack CLI login must authorize the CLI against the development workspace.
Workspace policy may require administrator approval before app creation or installation succeeds.

platform_outcomes.description: Slack creates and installs the app from the starter manifest when slack run selects Create a new app.
The running Bolt app responds to the hello message and Click Me button.

excluded.item: Editing the app to respond to goodbye
excluded.reason: The quickstart places farewell customization after the declared getting-started success.

excluded.item: Browser manifest, app-level token, and bot-token environment setup
excluded.reason: That path is the non-recommended alternative to slack run.

excluded.item: Reading Slack documentation as a counted action
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product interaction.

sources.title: Quickstart: Creating a Slack app
evidence_supported: slack create, venv, slack run, and hello / Click Me terminal

sources.title: Slack developer docs home
evidence_supported: Quickstart entry for building a first Slack app
Slack CLI recommended for scaffold, run, install, and lifecycle

uncertainties.question: What exact Slack account and workspace-creation fields does a brand-new developer complete before slack login?
uncertainties.impact: The audit boundary starts at account creation, but field counts for that gate remain unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough of Slack user signup and new workspace or sandbox creation through first CLI authentication.

uncertainties.question: Will the target workspace allow app creation and installation without administrator approval?
uncertainties.impact: Enterprise or locked-down workspace policy can insert an approval gate before slack run succeeds.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Confirm the measured path assumes a developer-owned sandbox where the operator can create apps without approval, or document the approval gate explicitly.
